page: Amazon Web Services c5.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8275CL processor running at 3.4 GHz. The configuration includes 8 vCPUs, which correspond to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU is equipped with 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 35 MB of L3 cache, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA.

The instance is provisioned with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs. Network connectivity is limited to a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most compute and memory operations. The stress-ng single-core score is 1494.09, and the multi-core score is 7865.45. Memory bandwidth tests show 437.77 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 50.41 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. While static web serving throughput reaches 7.26 GB/sec and Redis SET operations achieve 7.77 million operations per second, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and image processing are weak. Large-model LLM inference is a significant bottleneck, with 7B model prompt processing scoring in the bottom 10% at 11.09 tokens per second. This hardware profile is best suited for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, and web serving, rather than memory-intensive databases or modern AI inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c5.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 8 vCPUs (4 physical cores) clocked at 3.4 GHz, alongside 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks place the server in the average tier for most compute tasks, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1175.0 and multi-core score of 4531.0. However, it exhibits weaknesses in multi-threaded decompression and image processing, and shows poor performance in LLM inference. Qualitatively, the server offers a balanced compute-to-memory ratio for standard processing tasks but presents a clear tradeoff in memory-bound or storage-heavy scenarios. It is best suited for workloads like software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 8 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor, designed for standard compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and balanced DDR4 memory for standard compute-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
16.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz with no local storage
Nitro hypervisor with 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average performance in software compilation and ray tracing benchmarks
Weak performance in multi-threaded decompression and LLM inference
Best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and general compute tasks
